title: Parastrephia quadrangularis
intro: Parastrephia quadrangularis, also known as 'quadrangular bur marigold', is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Parastrephia quadrangularis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Parastrephia quadrangularis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Parastrephia quadrangularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Andean region and belongs to the genus Parastrephia. The classification of Parastrephia quadrangularis includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, order as Asterales, family as Asteraceae, genus as Parastrephia, and species as P. quadrangularis. This plant is also known as the 'huacarí' in the local Quechua language. Further research on this species may provide more detailed information on its classification and characteristics.
